lesson.menu_desc: Past Simple regular verbs -- describing past events -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- What Happened? (Past Simple)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael Gasparelli Lima | Aula 13 -- What Happened?

listenings.text: Good afternoon, everyone, and thank you for joining this quarterly update. Last quarter was a strong one for the company. In January, we launched a new service for our corporate clients. In February, the board signed an important contract with a partner in New York. We also hired four new lawyers for the team, and we opened a small office in Rio. The numbers improved month after month. By March, we finished every project on time and delivered all the reports to the clients. We achieved our main goal for the quarter. Thank you for your hard work, and let us continue this progress.

Two years ago, I started in this company as a junior director, and the first year was very hard. I remember the day we signed our first big contract. My hands shook, but I stayed calm. That month, we hired two people, and I trained them myself. We worked late, we finished every deadline, and slowly the results improved. Last quarter, we launched the project that everyone said was impossible. We delivered it on time, and the client thanked us in person. When I look back, I do not see luck. I see the days we planned, the calls we answered, and the small steps we completed, one by one.

extra_audio.text: First, in January, the company launched a new service for corporate clients. Then, in February, the board signed an important contract with a partner in New York. Next, the team hired four new lawyers and opened a small office in Rio. After that, they finished every project on time and delivered all the reports. Finally, the company achieved its main goal for the quarter.
